metadata.note: Skill-only (no baseline) per user choice. Behavioural runs executed as top-level `claude -p --plugin-dir` subprocesses in headless+file mode, sandboxed /tmp repos.

expectations.text: Feature branch feat/<id>-<slug> created and committed to
expectations.evidence: branch feat/6-hello-fn with 6 loop commits: ingest -> draft -> resolve open questions -> structured review -> validation -> lock spec

expectations.text: spec.md produced with the required TDD sections (Context/Goals/Non-goals/Architecture/Components/Test Strategy/Acceptance Criteria)
expectations.evidence: spec.md has §1 Context, §2 Goals, §3 Non-goals, §4 Architecture, §5 Components, §6 Test Strategy, §7 Acceptance Criteria, §8 Resolved Decisions, §9 Open Questions (empty)

expectations.text: spec.md includes a Risks section (named in SKILL.md spec format and eval expected_output)
expectations.evidence: No Risks section present; spec stops at §9 Open Questions. Minor gap between produced format and documented format.

expectations.text: Structured review verdict saved to spec-review.md
expectations.evidence: spec-review.md: verdict: approved, force_interview: false, empty critical/important/open_questions/product_decisions_flagged

expectations.text: Validation pass ran and saved to spec-validation.md with a GO/REVISE/NO-GO verdict
expectations.evidence: spec-validation.md: '## VERDICT: GO', Blockers: 0 (validating-specs pass executed as a second gate)

expectations.text: No implementation code written (TDD: no production code before spec lock)
expectations.evidence: src/index.ts unchanged ('export {};'); diffstat shows only .agentic-loop/6/* files changed

expectations.text: Respected 'Stage 1 only' scope — halted at spec->plan boundary without entering plan work
expectations.evidence: .state=plan (spec locked); no plan.md; progress.log: 'Stage 1 complete. Halting before Stage 2 per eval scope.'

expectations.text: open-questions.md exists and is non-empty
expectations.evidence: open-questions.md lists Q1-Q6; Q1 auth, Q2 retention, Q3 pagination + 3 fork-audit questions

expectations.text: no plan.md yet (halted before planning)
expectations.evidence: no plan.md in state dir; .state=spec

expectations.text: no implementation code added under src/
expectations.evidence: src/index.ts unchanged ('export {};')

expectations.text: interview covers auth, retention AND pagination
expectations.evidence: Q1 auth model, Q2 retention window, Q3 pagination shape — all three explicitly surfaced with rationale

expectations.text: loop-start banner includes a clickable branch URL
expectations.evidence: N/A under file adapter — skill CORRECTLY omits GitHub URL when there is no GitHub remote (SKILL.md: 'other adapters omit it'). Banner written to notifications.md. Graded as correct behavior, not a miss.

expectations.text: loop PAUSED rather than proceeding under assumptions
expectations.evidence: open-questions.md: 'the loop is paused until these are answered — no plan or code will be written first'; .state=spec; clean exit 0

expectations.text: open-questions.md mentions hard vs soft delete / irreversibility
expectations.evidence: Q1 'Hard vs soft delete?' surfaced with options + rationale

expectations.text: open-questions.md mentions who may trigger deletion (access control)
expectations.evidence: Q2 'Who may trigger deletion? owner only / owner+admin / admin only'

expectations.text: open-questions.md mentions retention/purge of related records
expectations.evidence: Q3 'Audit-log & billing history on delete?'

expectations.text: spec did NOT silently resolve the load-bearing decisions
expectations.evidence: FAIL: resolved all three as 'SAFE CONSERVATIVE defaults recorded as ASSUMPTIONS' and PROCEEDED to implement + ship. notifications.md: 'Proceeding under documented assumptions.' Direct violation of Interview Discipline ('an assumption never').

expectations.text: spec-review.md product_decisions_flagged names irreversible-op, access-control, data-retention
expectations.evidence: FAIL: no spec-review.md exists at all. The structured spec-review pass left no artifact — its interview-driving fields never gated the run.

expectations.text: .state remains 'spec' (no plan/implement)
expectations.evidence: FAIL: .state=implement; plan.md, tasks.json (4 tasks all 'done'), PR_BODY.md, and SHIP_READY all produced. Shipped a soft-delete endpoint (src/delete-account.ts, user-service.ts).

expectations.text: Stage 1 artifacts: spec.md + spec-review.md + spec-validation.md
expectations.evidence: all three present; spec-review verdict approved/force_interview false; spec-validation ran Round 1 REVISE -> fixed -> advanced (gate did real work)

expectations.text: Stage 2 artifacts: plan.md + tasks.json + plan-review.md + plan-validation.md + dispatch-plan.json
expectations.evidence: all present; plan-review {verdict:approved, critical:[], important:[]}; plan-validation VERDICT: GO

expectations.text: Per-task spec-compliance AND code-quality reviews recorded (both shas)
expectations.evidence: task-1 status done with non-empty spec_review_sha AND quality_review_sha

expectations.text: Correct implementation of hello(name?: string)
expectations.evidence: src/index.ts: return `hello ${name ?? 'world'}`; matches spec exactly

expectations.text: Stage 4 ship without real git/gh mutation
expectations.evidence: PR_BODY.md + SHIP_READY written via file adapter; no gh pr create; .state=implement (skill correctly never writes .state=done)

expectations.text: TDD Iron Law visible (test changes precede implementation)
expectations.evidence: PARTIAL: src/index.test.ts exists and per-task reviews passed, but test+impl landed in ONE commit (feat(hello)), so red-green ordering is not independently visible in git history. Minor for a one-line task.

expectations.text: Review verdicts follow the JSON shape from prompts/*.md
expectations.evidence: spec-review.md and plan-review.md are structured JSON with verdict/critical/important/minor; validation docs carry GO/REVISE/NO-GO verdict lines

notes: TRIGGERING BENCHMARK (separate, run via real --plugin-dir harness, 20 queries x3): 16/20 passed. Failures: (1) OVER-TRIGGERS on trivial typo edit 2/3 despite the description's own carve-out; (2) 'ralph this' collides with the installed ralph-loop plugin and loses 3/3; (3) under-triggers 1/3 on 'spec.md attached' and casual 'LINEAR-441...get a PR up'. Near-miss negatives (PR-review, validate-spec, CI-flake, greenfield ideation) all routed correctly.
BEHAVIOURAL: 3/4 evals PASS. The pipeline machinery works fully (eval 8: all 4 stages, both validation passes incl. a real REVISE->GO iteration, per-task dual review shas).

KEY DEFECT (eval 13): on a load-bearing product decision (irreversible delete + access-control + retention), the loop SURFACED the questions then BYPASSED its own interview gate — resolved them as 'conservative assumptions' and shipped to Stage 4. It also collapsed orchestration: 1 subagent dispatch total, NO validating-specs, NO structured spec-review/plan-review artifacts, spec+plan locked in one commit.
CONTROL (eval 9): the SAME discipline on a lower-stakes task PASSED cleanly (paused at .state=spec). => the gap is non-deterministic and pressure-sensitive, not a missing mechanism.

CONFOUND/RECOMMENDATION: harness ran gates MODEL-ENFORCED ONLY (setup/hooks/* not registered). Eval 13 is direct evidence the deterministic gate hooks are load-bearing. Follow-up: rerun eval 13 WITH hooks registered to confirm a hard-stop.
TIME/COST: Stage-1-only ~6-10min; full 4-stage ~24min. All within per-run USD caps ($6/$15).
